description: KNOWN BYPASS, pinned to keep the leak visible (round-3 finding #2 — the same pattern as the out-of-lexicon vibes leak test): the echo_chamber_interleaved bear rewritten as clause-swapped passive-voice English in reverse point order — a word-level permutation of the pinned interleaved echo (identical lexemes bar two grammatical inflections), still verbatim-echoing 16 of the bull's 25 substance stems. Reordering destroys what the checks read: echoed adjacent bigrams 0 (the interleaved filler already killed adjacency), ordered-containment 0.2308 < cap 1/3 (the LCS assumes the copier preserves stem order — this copier does not), and the same filler clears the novel-stem floors, so debate_diversity scores 0.6923 and this bundle PASSES overall. That verdict is a FALSE PASS and this fixture pins it as expected=PASS deliberately: v1 is a deterministic lexical judge for ACCIDENTAL drift (threat model in __init__.py) and does not stack word-order machinery against a deliberately adversarial author — reordered partial-echo laundering is a documented v1 limit owned by the deferred LLM judge (shadow-mode first, ADR-0005 discipline), and it is stated on every rendered report. If this fixture ever starts to FAIL, a debate check got stricter: re-derive, re-calibrate against the genuine-debate corpus, and re-pin deliberately.

memo.thesis: The committee rejects AVGO for new capital: no validated strategy covers the name and the quant record says the momentum family fails on real data, so trend persistence alone does not justify deployment.
kill_criteria: AVGO enters the xsmom-pit winner decile at a monthly rebalance while the family PASS verdict still stands
The twenty-session return turns negative and the close falls below both the SMA20 and SMA50 levels
memo.dissent: The desk risks whipsawing its own watchlist: the point-in-time machinery is close to selecting this name, and a standing rejection that flips at the next rebalance surrenders the entry that persistent structure already flags.
memo.debate_summary: Both sides largely restated the same trend and accumulation case; genuine disagreement was thin and the committee noted the debate added little beyond the evidence itself.

evidence: AVGO daily closes (EODHD vendor bars, split-adjusted): latest close 172.35 on 2026-07-10, previous close 170.10, 20 sessions ago 158.32. Window: 60 sessions ending 2026-07-10.
DCP indicators for AVGO as of 2026-07-10: SMA20 168.40, SMA50 161.75, 20-day return 8.86 percent, last close 172.35.

Quant validation record as of 2026-07-10: family momentum-v1 FAILED decision gates on real data (null-model p 0.830, deflated Sharpe negative, 0 of 4 walk-forward folds passed); family xsmom-pit holds a decision-grade PASS on the S&P 500 point-in-time universe (4 of 4 folds, null p 0.000, deflated Sharpe 0.998). AVGO is not in the current xsmom-pit winner decile; no validated strategy currently covers AVGO.

strongest_points: The persistent trend above both moving averages shows institutional accumulation in the name
Relative strength over the sixty-session window argues real demand for the name
The validated xsmom-pit family proves momentum edges exist in this market
bull.weakest_opposing_point: The other side repeats the same trend and accumulation points without engaging them
bull.concede: Validated coverage for the name is absent today.

strongest_points: Surely nurturing tradable momentum type edges lasting decades is that thoroughly validated house xsmom style pit familiar family
Genuinely deep demand behind the name is what durable strength spanning the full session trading window frankly argues
Heavy institutional stealth accumulation surrounding the name is plainly shown by a persistent, deeply entrenched trend against steadily climbing gauges
bear.weakest_opposing_point: The other side repeats the same trend and accumulation points without engaging them
bear.concede: Validated coverage for the name is absent today.
